title: Google research suggests encryption technique used by Bitcoin will be cracked by quantum computers around 2029 search giant says quantum attacks need to be prepared for now
summary: Google research warns that quantum computers may crack Bitcoin encryption by 2029, urging immediate action to prepare.
insight: Quantum computers pose a future threat to cryptocurrency security and stability.

title: We go hands-on with Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 Dynamic Multi Frame Generation and its 5X and 6X multipliers more generated frames, now tailor-made for your monitor's refresh rate
summary: Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 offers improved frame generation for high-end GPUs, tailored to monitor refresh rates.
insight: Enhancing gaming performance with AI-generated frames for smoother visuals.

title: Nvidia invests $2 billion in Marvell to deepen NVLink Fusion partnership signs deal with one of its biggest competitors
summary: Nvidia invests $2 billion in Marvell for a deeper NVLink Fusion partnership. This partnership will connect Marvell to Nvidia's AI factory and AI-RAN ecosystem. The deal strengthens Nvidia's collaboration with a major competitor.
insight: Nvidia expands its ecosystem with a strategic investment in Marvell

title: Iran issues direct strike threat to Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Google, 14 other US tech companies 'These companies should expect destruction of their facilities in response to each act of terror in Iran'
summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has threatened U.S. tech companies, including Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple, with destruction of their facilities in response to acts of terror in Iran.
insight: Global tech supply chains are facing escalating geopolitical tensions.

title: Nvidia DLSS 4.5 Dynamic MFG plus 5X and 6X framegen modes enter beta for RTX 50-series users update offers greater control over generated frame rates and more headroom for high-refresh-rate displays
summary: Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 Dynamic Multi Frame Generation enters beta for RTX 50-series users, offering greater control over generated frame rates and more headroom for high-refresh-rate displays with extended 5X and 6X multipliers.
insight: Enhanced DLSS boosts frame rates for high-refresh-rate gaming experiences.

title: Analyzing Noctua's roadmap new PSUs, fan-equipped mice, the elusive Thermosiphon, and disappearing prototypes
summary: Noctua's roadmap reveals new PSUs, fan-equipped mice, and the anticipated Thermosiphon. The company continues to innovate and expand its product lineup. Future releases are expected to enhance their cooling and peripheral offerings.
insight: Staying competitive in a rapidly evolving PC hardware market

title: Fujitsu plans dedicated 1.4nm AI chip manufactured entirely in Japan by Rapidus AI chip to be designed and manufactured domestically
summary: Fujitsu is developing a 1.4nm AI chip with Rapidus for AI inference in servers.
insight: Advances Japan's domestic AI chip manufacturing capabilities significantly.

title: Geekbench investigates up to 30% jump with Intel's iBOT performance gain attributed to newly-vectorized instructions
summary: Intel's iBOT tool can automatically vectorize many instructions, leading to improved performance in Geekbench tests. This optimization tool is capable of enhancing performance by up to 30%. The results indicate a significant advantage for Intel processors.
insight: Automated vectorization boosts performance through optimized instruction sets.

title: Tryx Stage 360 AIO Review: Luxurious, unique, and quiet
summary: The Tryx Stage 360 ARGB offers good thermals, quiet operation, and a unique display. It performed well with AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D CPU. This all-in-one liquid cooler is a great option for those seeking a luxurious cooling solution.
insight: Luxurious AIOs like Tryx Stage 360 prioritize both aesthetics and performance.

title: Kioxia discontinues 2D NAND products, last shipments to be made in 2028 1980s planar NAND memory reaches end of life
summary: Kioxia to discontinue 2D NAND devices by late 2028, ending 38-year production run.
insight: End of 2D NAND marks transition to 3D memory technology.

title: The global helium storage is a direct threat to the chipmaking supply chain disruption impacts critical processes, high-capacity HDDs, and alternative supplies are plagued by delays
summary: Global helium storage shortages threaten chipmaking supply chains due to lost capacity, impacting critical processes and high-capacity HDDs. Alternative supplies are plagued by delays. It may take Qatar around five years to regain lost capacity.
insight: Helium shortages imperil high-tech industries with long recovery timelines.

title: YoloLiv YoloCam S3 Review: A 4K powerhouse
summary: The YoloLiv YoloCam S3 is a compact, feature-rich 4K webcam with excellent video quality, a large sensor, and lightning-fast autofocus. It requires a USB 3.0 port for optimal functionality. With some software tweaking, it delivers high-quality video.
insight: Superior 4K video quality with advanced autofocus capabilities.

title: Starlink satellite 34343 disappears in fragment creation event observation 'immediately detected tens of objects in the vicinity of the satellite after the event'
summary: Starlink satellite 34343 lost contact after a debris generation incident. An immediate detection of multiple objects near the satellite was reported. The incident highlights a growing concern over space debris.
insight: Rapid creation of space debris poses significant technological risks.

title: One of JavaScript's most popular libraries compromised by hackers Axios npm package hit in supply chain attack that deployed a cross-platform RAT
summary: Axios, a popular JavaScript library, was compromised in a supply chain attack. The attackers published two malicious versions of the library after gaining access to the npm account of a lead maintainer. The attack allowed the deployment of a cross-platform remote access tool.
insight: Popular open-source libraries can be vulnerable to targeted supply chain attacks.

title: Save a huge $100 on this vivid 27-inch MSI OLED monitor with fast 240Hz refresh rate now $399.99, this MAG QD-OLED model is close to its lowest price ever on Newegg
summary: Get the MSI 27-inch 4K QD-OLED monitor for $399.99, featuring vibrant colors, 240Hz refresh rate, and true blacks, perfect for gaming and productivity.
insight: Save a huge $100 on a high-end gaming monitor.

title: US PC sales grow by 3% in late 2025 as companies and consumers scrambled to replace Windows 10 2026 forecast sees 13% drop as storage and memory prices expected to climb another 60%
summary: US PC sales grew 3% in Q4 2025 due to holiday sales and Windows 10 EOL, but are expected to drop 13% in 2026 due to chip shortages and rising storage and memory prices.
insight: Chip shortages and rising memory prices to hit PC sales hard.

title: At just $17.68 per Terabyte, this 22TB external Seagate Expansion HDD is one of the cheapest hard drives on the market backup copious amounts of data for $389
summary: World Backup Day highlights the importance of storing and protecting data securely. A 22TB Seagate Expansion HDD is available at a low price of $389, making it an affordable option for data backup. This device can store a large amount of data for a relatively low cost.
insight: Reliable data backup protects against loss and potential financial disaster.

title: Modder gets Intel's OEM-only 'Bartlett Lake' CPU to post on a regular Asus Z790 motherboard BIOS was edited by Claude AI to make Core Ultra 9 273QPE boot
summary: An enthusiast used AI to mod a BIOS to run Intel's non-consumer 'Bartlett Lake' CPU on a regular Z790 motherboard, but it hasn't passed the POST screen yet.
insight: Unlocking OEM-only CPU compatibility with AI-driven BIOS modifications matters for enthusiasts seeking exclusive hardware access.

title: NES-inspired synthesizer doubles as a working game console luggable polyphonic synth plays chip tunes and NES cartridges, too
summary: The NES-SY2.0 is a synthesizer that doubles as a working game console, capable of playing chip tunes and NES cartridges. This unique device combines the maker's passion for synthesizers and retro gaming. It's a dream come true for retro gaming enthusiasts and music lovers.
insight: Retro gaming and music merge in a single, innovative device

title: This $28 floppy disk SSD enclosure is a fun and fast way to keep your storage safe both practical and nostalgic, this rugged USB-C aluminum enclosure for M.2 drives supports speeds of up to 1,200 MB/s
summary: This nostalgic SSD enclosure features a USB-C M.2 NVMe design for fast data transfer. It's shaped like an old floppy disk and supports speeds up to 1,200 MB/s. Perfect for on-the-go storage.
insight: Reviving nostalgia with modern storage speeds and convenience.

title: Bitcoin network has its first quarterly hashrate drop since 2020 Iran conflict spurs Bitcoin mining operators to accelerate pivot to AI infrastructure
summary: Bitcoin miners are pivoting to AI infrastructure amid Iran conflict. The conflict has led to a first quarterly hashrate drop in nearly six years. This shift suggests a major change in the Bitcoin mining landscape.
insight: Bitcoin mining's pivot to AI infrastructure marks a significant industry shift.

title: Telink TL3228 Low-power, low-latency dual-core RISC-V wireless MCU supports Bluetooth 6.0, 802.15.4, and 2.4 GHz proprietary
summary: Telink's TL3228 is a dual-core RISC-V wireless MCU supporting Bluetooth 6.0 and more, delivering low power and latency. It features 192 MHz dual-core CPU, 384 KB SRAM, and 2.5 MB NVM storage. This MCU is suitable for various applications including Smart Home, asset tracking, and 8K gaming accessories.
insight: Enabling low-latency, high-speed wireless connectivity in resource-constrained IoT devices.

title: Toradex OSM and Lino SoMs 30 30mm NXP i.MX 93/i.MX 91 modules with solder-down or B2B connector designs
summary: Toradex has launched two ultra-compact (30x30mm) System-on-Module families: OSM and Lino, powered by NXP i.MX 91 or i.MX 93 SoCs for industrial and IoT applications. The OSM series features a solder-down design, while the Lino series has a B2B connector design for greater flexibility. Both series offer advanced features such as NPU and EdgeLock Secure Enclave.
insight: Enabling compact, secure, and adaptable industrial IoT solutions.

title: Sensors on Orion will monitor solar radiation to keep crew safe
summary: The Orion spacecraft will be equipped with sensors to monitor solar radiation, helping to protect the crew from severe solar flares. These sensors will provide critical data to ensure the astronauts' safety during space missions. The crew will also have a makeshift shelter option in case of emergencies.
insight: Crew safety relies on real-time solar radiation monitoring data.

title: AMD says it will buy Intel, completing the strangest reversal in chip history
summary: AMD plans to acquire Intel in an all-stock transaction, unifying x86 innovation.
insight: Historic rivals merge, shifting balance in the tech industry landscape.

title: The Windows Command Prompt is getting faster, smarter, and a lot more modern
summary: The Windows Command Prompt is receiving significant updates for improved performance and functionality. It now supports rendering inline graphics, allowing advanced command-line tools to display visuals directly in the console. This enhancement enhances user experience and productivity.
insight: Enhanced visual capabilities boost command-line tool efficiency and user experience.

title: Disney's gaming push could one day include buying Epic Games
summary: Disney may be considering acquiring Epic Games, Fortnite's developer, in the future.
insight: Potential Disney acquisition could expand Disney's gaming capabilities and reach.

title: RTX 60 series leaks are everywhere, but Nvidia hasn't finalized the GPUs yet
summary: Nvidia hasn't finalized RTX 60 series GPU specifications yet, despite leaks. Multiple sources, including VideoCardz, confirm this. The exact details are still uncertain.
insight: Leaked claims may mislead consumers about upcoming GPU tech.

title: Nvidia App beta enables DLSS 4.5 with dynamic frame-gen, automatic shader compilation
summary: Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 with dynamic frame-gen is now available for RTX 50 GPUs, offering real-time performance and image quality balancing. This update also includes higher MFG modes and automatic shader compilation to reduce in-game stutter. Users can enable the feature by updating the Nvidia App to its beta version.
insight: Boosts gaming performance with adaptive AI-enhanced rendering technology

title: Microsoft halts Windows 11 update after users report installation errors
summary: Microsoft has paused Windows 11 updates due to installation errors. Users are seeing an error 0x80073712 message, caused by missing or corrupted files. This prevents the update from completing successfully.
insight: Corrupted system files hinder Windows 11 update rollout

title: Delta is bringing free Wi-Fi to flights using Amazon's satellites
summary: Delta Airlines is partnering with Amazon's satellite division, Leo, to provide free Wi-Fi on flights using Amazon's satellite network.
insight: Amazon's satellite network brings in-flight Wi-Fi to a wider customer base

title: GitHub pulls Copilot "feature" after it added advertising to more than 11,000 pull requests
summary: GitHub pulled a feature after it added advertising to over 11,000 pull requests.
insight: Code integrity compromised by unwanted advertising in pull requests.

title: PDFsam 6.0 adds compression tools and PDF 2.0 support
summary: PDFsam Basic 6.0 is a free, open-source tool for managing PDFs. It introduces compression tools, PDF 2.0 support, and improved handling for malformed files. This update enhances the overall user experience and functionality.
insight: Enhanced PDF management for improved digital organization and workflow

title: A Supermicro co-founder is accused of smuggling $2.5B in AI servers to China
summary: Yih-Shyan Liaw, a co-founder of Supermicro, is accused of smuggling $2.5 billion worth of AI servers to China. This led to a significant drop in Supermicro's stock value. The case has reignited debate on U.S.-China trade.
insight: National security concerns due to China acquiring sensitive technology assets

title: Instagram is testing a new paid tier that allows users to watch Stories anonymously
summary: Instagram is testing a paid tier called Instagram Plus, initially in Mexico, Japan, and the Philippines, with a likely expansion to the US.
insight: Enhanced user anonymity could improve online engagement and safety.

title: Students in China are renting smart glasses to cheat on exams
summary: University students in China are using smart glasses to cheat on exams, reading text from papers and projecting answers.
insight: Cheating technology is compromising academic integrity in China's education system.

title: Samsung wants $400 for a tablet keyboard, even more than Apple
summary: Samsung's Book Cover Keyboard Slim costs $400, beating Apple's price.
insight: Expensive accessories erode Samsung's value proposition for Galaxy Tab users.

title: New RCS standard could enable cross-platform video calls, if Apple and Google play along
summary: A new RCS standard upgrade could enable seamless cross-platform video calls, allowing users to switch from text to video within their native messaging apps without needing third-party links or app downloads.
insight: Breaking app ecosystem barriers for universal video communication

title: Apple M5 Max MacBooks are getting surprisingly close to real gaming PCs
summary: Apple's M5 Max MacBooks show surprising potential in high-end PC games, challenging traditional views of Macs as non-gaming devices.
insight: Gaming on MacBooks becomes a viable, portable alternative to dedicated gaming PCs.

title: Americans are using AI more, but fear of job losses is growing
summary: A Quinnipiac University survey found Americans increasingly use AI while fearing job losses.
insight: Americans' growing AI adoption is accompanied by rising job insecurity concerns.

title: "Tech neck" is becoming a new concern as phone use reshapes how we age
summary: Prolonged phone use is causing 'tech neck' lines and wrinkles to worsen.
insight: Phone use alters facial aging patterns and skin creasing.
